tasks.method: Execute a pointer-chasing kernel with minimal compute overhead and use hardware cycle counters to measure the elapsed time between consecutive memory accesses, then normalize by the GPU clock frequency to obtain latency in cycles.

task.method: Execute a pointer-chasing kernel with minimal compute overhead and use hardware cycle counters to measure the elapsed time between consecutive memory accesses, then normalize by the GPU clock frequency to obtain latency in cycles.

tasks.method: Execute a pointer-chasing microbenchmark with cache eviction to force DRAM accesses, then measure the round-trip time using hardware performance counters and convert it to clock cycles based on the GPU memory clock frequency.

task.method: Execute a pointer-chasing microbenchmark with cache eviction to force DRAM accesses, then measure the round-trip time using hardware performance counters and convert it to clock cycles based on the GPU memory clock frequency.

tasks.method: pointer-chasing with random permutation using clock64() to measure elapsed cycles; allocate a large working set (e.g., 128 MB) exceeding L2 cache size to ensure DRAM access; traverse a linked list with randomized indices to prevent prefetching; measure round-trip latency per access and compute median over 3 trials

task.method: pointer-chasing with random permutation using clock64() to measure elapsed cycles; allocate a large working set (e.g., 128 MB) exceeding L2 cache size to ensure DRAM access; traverse a linked list with randomized indices to prevent prefetching; measure round-trip latency per access and compute median over 3 trials

tasks.method: pointer-chasing with random permutation to traverse a large linked list (working set > L2 cache size, e.g., 128 MB) allocated in global memory. Each node contains a pointer to the next node, and the kernel performs a sequence of dependent loads. Use clock64() to measure the total cycles for a fixed number of hops (e.g., 1M), then divide by the number of hops to get average latency per access. Repeat 3 times and report median to reduce noise from system interference.

task.method: pointer-chasing with random permutation to traverse a large linked list (working set > L2 cache size, e.g., 128 MB) allocated in global memory. Each node contains a pointer to the next node, and the kernel performs a sequence of dependent loads. Use clock64() to measure the total cycles for a fixed number of hops (e.g., 1M), then divide by the number of hops to get average latency per access. Repeat 3 times and report median to reduce noise from system interference.

tasks.method: Use pointer-chasing with a random permutation of memory addresses to measure DRAM access latency. Allocate a working set significantly larger than the L2 cache (e.g., 128 MB) to ensure accesses bypass cache and reach DRAM. Traverse the linked list using clock64() to record start and end timestamps for each access, then compute the average latency in cycles across multiple trials (recommend 3 trials, report median). The pointer chain is constructed using a pseudo-random sequence to prevent prefetcher optimization.

task.method: Use pointer-chasing with a random permutation of memory addresses to measure DRAM access latency. Allocate a working set significantly larger than the L2 cache (e.g., 128 MB) to ensure accesses bypass cache and reach DRAM. Traverse the linked list using clock64() to record start and end timestamps for each access, then compute the average latency in cycles across multiple trials (recommend 3 trials, report median). The pointer chain is constructed using a pseudo-random sequence to prevent prefetcher optimization.

tasks.method: Measure DRAM access latency using a pointer-chasing micro-benchmark with a large working set (e.g., 128 MB) to ensure data resides in DRAM and not in caches. Traverse a linked list with random node order to prevent prefetching optimizations. Use clock64() to time individual memory accesses, recording the number of cycles per access. Perform multiple trials (e.g., 3) and report the median latency in GPU cycles to ensure statistical confidence.

task.method: Measure DRAM access latency using a pointer-chasing micro-benchmark with a large working set (e.g., 128 MB) to ensure data resides in DRAM and not in caches. Traverse a linked list with random node order to prevent prefetching optimizations. Use clock64() to time individual memory accesses, recording the number of cycles per access. Perform multiple trials (e.g., 3) and report the median latency in GPU cycles to ensure statistical confidence.
